content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum                                M-N19-061

To    : File                                Date  :Sept. 24, 1979


From  : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
         John Giansello

Subject:


Mr. William Kirby, co-treasurer of Quentin Kopp's Campaign for
Mayor of San Francisco, called today (Sept. 21, 1979). Mr. Kopp
has a controlled committee, Friends of Kopp, which was formed
for and has devoted itself entirely to keeping Supervisor Kopp
in touch with his district constituency. It does not participate
and has not participated in the mayoral campaign. Mr. Kirby asks
whether Friends of Kopp must report on the mayoral election
schedule or whether it need only file semi-annual reports.

After consultation with Bob Stern I determined that Friends of
Kopp is a "committee supporting or opposing a candidate" by the
terms of Government Code Section 84200 and so must file under that
section in connection with the election. I have so informed
Mr. Kirby.

We have further agreed that the non-involvement in the mayoral
campaign of Friends of Kopp and the strict separation of its funds
from mayoral transactions are not material to disposition of the
issue. The significant fact in this case is that the office in
which Mr. Kopp is an incumbent, supervisor, and the office which
he is seeking, mayor, are both offices in the same jurisdiction,
the City and County of San Francisco.

Were Mr. Kopp an incumbent in one jurisdiction, for example, as a
city councilman, seeking an office in another jurisdiction, for
example, a seat in the state legislature, our advice might well
be different. We might conclude that a committee formed for the
purpose of supporting the incumbent in his office of incumbency
and confining its activities to that purpose and having no acti-
vity during the period of campaigning for the office in another
jurisdiction need not file on the election schedule for the latter
office but might file only semi-annual reports as required by
Section 84206.


cc: Bob Stern
    Gina
    Jay
    John K.

sections.question: Whether Friends of Kopp must report on the mayoral election schedule or only file semi-annual reports.
sections.conclusion: Friends of Kopp is a "committee supporting or opposing a candidate" under Government Code Section 84200 and must file under that section in connection with the mayoral election. The non-involvement in the mayoral campaign and strict separation of funds are not material; the significant fact is that both the supervisor office (incumbent) and mayor office (sought) are in the same jurisdiction (City and County of San Francisco).
sections.question_synthetic: Mr. William Kirby inquired whether Friends of Kopp, a committee formed by Quentin Kopp for his supervisor campaign, must file on the mayoral election schedule or only file semi-annual reports for Mr. Kopp's mayoral candidacy.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC determined that Friends of Kopp must file on the mayoral election schedule under Government Code Section 84200 because both offices are within the same jurisdiction, regardless of the committee's non-involvement in the mayoral campaign or fund separation. The advice would differ if the incumbent and sought offices were in different jurisdictions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers but contains clear inquiry and advice determination. Hypothetical scenario included regarding different jurisdictions. Internal routing notation suggests this is memorandum to file for administrative record.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Friends of Kopp must report on the mayoral election schedule or only file semi-annual reports.

CONCLUSION: Friends of Kopp is a "committee supporting or opposing a candidate" under Government Code Section 84200 and must file under that section in connection with the mayoral election. The non-involvement in the mayoral campaign and strict separation of funds are not material; the significant fact is that both the supervisor office (incumbent) and mayor office (sought) are in the same jurisdiction (City and County of San Francisco).
embedding.first_500_words: This memorandum, dated September 24, 1979, is from the Fair Political Practices Commission to File. It discusses the status of Friends of Kopp, a committee formed by Quentin Kopp for his campaign as Supervisor in San Francisco. Mr. William Kirby, co-treasurer of Quentin Kopp's Campaign for Mayor of San Francisco, called to inquire whether Friends of Kopp must report on the mayoral election schedule or only file semi-annual reports. The commission determined that Friends of Kopp is a "committee supporting or opposing a candidate" under Government Code Section 84200 and must file under that section in connection with the election. The commission also agreed that the non-involvement in the mayoral campaign of Friends of Kopp and the strict separation of its funds from mayoral transactions are not material to disposition of the issue. The significant fact is that both the office in which Mr. Kopp is an incumbent, supervisor, and the office he is seeking, mayor, are both offices in the same jurisdiction, the City and County of San Francisco. If Mr. Kopp were an incumbent in one jurisdiction, such as a city councilman, seeking an office in another jurisdiction, like a seat in the state legislature, the advice might be different. In such a case, the commission might conclude that a committee formed for the purpose of supporting the incumbent in his office of incumbency and confining its activities to that purpose and having no activity during the period of campaigning for the office in another jurisdiction need not file on the election schedule for the latter office but might file only semi-annual reports as required by Section 84206. cc: Bob Stern Gina Jay John K.
embedding.summary: This memorandum from the FPPC addresses whether Friends of Kopp must file on the mayoral election schedule or only file semi-annual reports for Quentin Kopp's mayoral candidacy. The FPPC concluded that filing on the election schedule is required under Government Code Section 84200 because both the supervisor position (incumbent) and mayor position (sought) are in the same jurisdiction.
